claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances.

Let me start with a few questions:

**Do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm responsible for one child. His name is Alex, and he's 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're responsible for Alex who is 15 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to understand your living arrangement with Alex:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! That's helpful information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in any education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to clarify a couple more things:

**Has Alex started paid work for 24 hours or more a week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work 24 hours or more a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex claiming any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't claiming any benefits in their own right.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16
- You live in the UK and Alex lives with you, meaning you're responsible for him
- Alex is not working 24+ hours per week
- Alex is not claiming any benefits in their own right

Since Alex is under 16, you qualify for Child Benefit. The requirement for approved education or training only applies once a child turns 16 or over.

**Important note:** When Alex turns 16, if you want to continue receiving Child Benefit, Alex will need to be in approved education or training. But for now, you meet all the eligibility criteria.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. Also, be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Alex lives with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for him. Alex is not working 24 hours or more a week and is not claiming benefits in their own right, so none of the disqualifying conditions apply. The fact that Alex is not currently in education or training does not affect eligibility at age 15 - the education/training requirement only becomes relevant when a child turns 16 or over. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for one child, Alex (age 15), who lives with them. Since Alex is under 16 and none of the disqualifying conditions apply (not working 24+ hours/week, not claiming benefits), the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

The fact that Alex is not currently in education or training does not affect eligibility at age 15 - the education/training requirement only becomes relevant when a child turns 16 or over. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is under 16 and none of the disqualifying conditions apply (not working 24+ hours/week, not claiming benefits), the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
